text.title: Serverless Architecture Foundations Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the serverless-foundations methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Workload MUST fit FaaS execution limits (AWS Lambda < 15min, < 10GB memory, payload < 6MB sync / 256KB async). Workloads exceeding limits MUST move to containers or Step Functions.

rationale.source: AWS Lambda quotas; cloud-provider FaaS reference.

rule.rationale: Hitting a limit produces silent truncation or timeouts in production; up-front check avoids re-architecture.

rule.statement: Functions MUST be stateless; any persistent or shared state MUST live in managed services (DB, KV, object store, queue). Local disk MAY be used as ephemeral scratch only.
rule.rationale: FaaS instances reuse memory across invocations; mutable global state produces cross-tenant leaks and non-deterministic bugs.

rule.statement: Each function MUST have one trigger and one responsibility. Multi-trigger 'kitchen-sink' functions MUST be split.
rule.rationale: Multi-trigger functions complicate cold-start, monitoring, and IAM scoping; single-responsibility keeps blast radius small.

rule.statement: Logs, metrics, and distributed traces MUST be wired with a correlation id propagated from trigger to downstream services. Console-only debugging MUST NOT be the primary feedback loop.
rule.rationale: Serverless decomposition makes call paths invisible without tracing; correlation ids enable incident analysis.

rule.statement: All serverless resources (functions, event sources, IAM, queues, tables) MUST be defined via IaC. Console-clicked stacks MUST NOT progress past prototype.
rule.rationale: Console state drifts and cannot be code-reviewed or rolled back atomically; IaC is the only auditable shape.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (workload description, provider, IAM, observability stack), walk r1 → r5 and mark each item with yes/no/na + rationale.
rule.rationale: Skipped items are the failure modes; the checklist is the gate.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) workload already runtime-mapped and not under review, (b) team beyond foundations bar, (c) throwaway script. Exit with note.
rule.rationale: Foundations checklist in these contexts wastes review time.
